chunks.text: The discovery of penicillin was the child of an accident.
In 1928, in a laboratory in London, a scientist returned from his vacation.
Alexander Fleming noticed that a strange mold had grown on one of the dishes left on his bench.
Around the mold, the bacteria had vanished.
If Fleming had missed that small observation, what the world would have lost!
If he had not noticed that mold, many infection might not have been treated
and countless lives might have been lost from wound on the battlefield.
Modern medicine was born from that one dish.
The quiet care of a single scientist saved millions of lives.
If only that chance had not arrived
the history of science would have been utterly different.
